_meta.channel_note: Day 0. Brian's account. Attach the branded social card (PNG) to the FIRST tweet at post time — Draft.media needs a PNG Buffer, which can't live in JSON. Alt text below. DRY_RUN=1 smoke first.
_meta.card_alt: HVTrust leaderboard card: Threadplane ranked #7 of 75 agent frameworks, trust score 82.8 out of 100, Grade A, shown above CrewAI and LangChain.

threadParts:
We just got independently graded A for trust.

82.8/100 — #7 of 75 agent frameworks on HVTracker, and the only Angular one.

Here's the part that still surprises me 🧵

We rank above CrewAI, AutoGen, and LangChain on trust.

With 99 GitHub stars against their tens of thousands.

That's not a glitch. HVTrust weights supply-chain integrity and provenance — not popularity.

Why it matters: an agent framework runs models, calls tools, reaches for credentials, and acts on your users' behalf.

The blast radius of a bad dependency under an agent is bigger than almost anything else you install.

What earned the grade is unglamorous:

• OSSF Scorecard 7.7/10
• MIT license
• 82% of recent commits signed
• verifiable npm provenance
• active maintenance

Boring hygiene, done consistently. All of it public.

And I'll name our weak spot: adoption.

99 stars is the one number we're behind on.

If the trust story lands for you, a star genuinely helps us close the gap we're honest about.

Read the full breakdown — and verify it yourself:

Blog → https://threadplane.ai/blog/threadplane-earns-grade-a-hvtrust?utm_source=x&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=grade-a-trust

Listing → https://hvtracker.net/agents/threadplane/
